expectations.text: Provides a numerical score for the original prompt with specific grades per dimension
expectations.evidence: Shows F (22/100) with per-dimension scores: Specificity 10/F, Clarity 25/F, Context 10/F, Completeness 15/F, Constraint Control 40/D, Actionability 30/F

expectations.text: Identifies the 2-3 weakest dimensions with specific feedback on why they are weak
expectations.evidence: Identifies Specificity (10), Context (10), Completeness (15) as top 3 weakest with detailed feedback

expectations.text: Recommends a specific prompting framework (e.g., RTF, SCOPE, TRACE) and explains why
expectations.evidence: Recommends SCOPE framework and explains: 'it forces specificity and context upfront, which are the two weakest dimensions'

expectations.text: Provides a complete rewritten prompt that is clearly better structured
expectations.evidence: Provides a full improved prompt with role assignment, endpoint details, required sections, style requirements, DO NOT list, and few-shot example

expectations.text: Shows before and after scores to quantify the improvement
expectations.evidence: Before: F (22/100) -> After: A (86/100), with full re-score breakdown showing each dimension improvement

expectations.text: Explicitly names the prompting techniques applied (e.g., Role Assignment, Few-Shot, Negative Constraint)
expectations.evidence: Names 6 techniques: Explicit Instruction, Role Assignment, Structured Output, Few-Shot Learning, Negative Constraint, SCOPE Framework
